mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals an impending economic contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict recessionary earnings misses.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with accommodative monetary policy erodes real household purchasing power and signals future policy tightening, which suppresses consumer demand and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power for forward returns when controlling for single-series effects, or forward returns are positive in over 50% of high-inflation/low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates supply chain normalization and capacity expansion, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates negatively with forward equity returns over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1.5 million units signals a sharp demand contraction in the construction sector, which leads equity markets downward over the next week as financials and materials sectors decline.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of instances where housing starts breach the threshold, or the signal exhibits no statistical edge over a random baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales revenue reflects robust consumer spending, which directly drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth shows a persistent negative correlation with forward equity returns across multiple business cycles, or the signal fails to generate positive alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth coinciding with accelerating inflation forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy longer, compressing equity multiples and reducing forward returns as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance in predicting forward returns, or equity multiples expand despite simultaneous strong payroll growth and high inflation.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, each successive rate hike reduces equity valuations as discount rates rise faster than earnings can adjust, producing negative short-term returns across broad market indices.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of rate hike months, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during periods of aggressive policy normalization.
